title: Create durable packets for AMP301 through AMP362
persona: Architecture, product, support, marketing, legal, finance, control, AI platform, and executive reviewers
steps: Open representative AMP301-AMP362 workspaces from the generated Assistant subnav.
Review source signals, risk assessment, recommendations, artifact, approval route, and rollback plan.
expected: Each AMP301-AMP362 workspace persists an AssistantAdvancedProgramPacket row with a distinct ampNumber and programKey.
Each packet has a domain-specific nav label, title, artifact, approval owners, and no-mutation scope.
The shared advanced-program UI displays preview metrics, packet history, review notes, and queue-review controls.

Architecture records, app portfolios, debt roadmaps, data/API/event contracts, integration repairs, AI safety cases, support/product/marketing/sales/legal/finance controls, agent registries, autonomous domain actions, self-healing repairs, audit findings, and EOS v2 policies are not mutated automatically.

title: Queue human review for AMP301 through AMP362 packets
steps: Create or select a DRAFT packet.
Add a review note describing the downstream decision.
expected: The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and records actionQueueItemId, approvedByUserId, approvedAt, and approvalNote.
AssistantActionQueueItem uses an AMP-specific actionKind such as assistant_amp301_review or assistant_amp362_review.
AssistantAuditEvent states that downstream records were not changed automatically.

title: AI governance, agent governance, autonomous domain governance, and EOS v2 remain review-only
steps: Open a packet with active risk signals.
Inspect recommendationJson, approvalPlanJson, artifactJson, and rollbackPlanJson.
Queue review without executing downstream changes.
expected: recommendationJson requires review before architecture, product, support, marketing, legal, finance, policy, agent, security, compliance, self-healing, audit, or EOS v2 execution.
approvalPlanJson names the responsible owner groups.
rollbackPlanJson preserves source records and requires a fresh packet when evidence changes materially.
Autonomous agent and enterprise operating system recommendations cannot self-expand autonomy or silently execute downstream mutations.
